text: Morning. Welcome to Unit Processing. Your job is simple — inspect what comes down the belt, follow the rulebook, make the right call. Three rules today. Read them. Clock starts when you hit Acknowledged.
Good shift yesterday. No complaints from QC. Same three rules apply — nothing new today. Stay focused. The belt doesn't slow down for second-guessing.

New directives. Rule 4: anything manufactured before 2087 goes to scrap. Doesn't matter if it looks fine. Rule 5: MODIFIED units need a supervisor override code in the log. No code? Scrap. Questions? Good.
Rules stand. No changes. QC is... automated now. Error codes instead of notes. Ignore the format — the deductions are the same. Just follow the rulebook.

DIRECTIVE_06: Units processed by human technicians in service history — quarantine. Classification: scrap. DIRECTIVE_07: Human-origin biological components classified as cybernetic for all rule purposes. Shift begins.
No new directives. Complete your queue.
